title: Integrated Support for Complex and High-Risk Needs
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeIntegrated Support for Complex and High-Risk Needs 1 minuteTraining & PDA structured clinical intervention for a client with justice involvement, chronic substance use, and cognitive complexity — resulting in ADHD diagnosis, reduced offending, and supported disability pathways.Background The client presented with long-standing and complex needs, including a history of institutionalisation across the criminal justice system, out-of-home care, and psychiatric services. At referral, the client was experiencing ongoing substance use, cognitive concerns, and challenging behaviours that significantly impacted their engagement with employment services.They were frequently missing appointments, attending sessions while substance-affected, and had no active case management or coordinated support in place within the greater community. Employment was unlikely but there was no progress in obtaining documentation to support disability pathways.Our ApproachUpon intake, our qualified clinician completed a Risk, Need and Capacity assessment, with risks clearly identified and service delivery modified accordingly — for example, larger rooms and flexible engagement strategies where there was a risk of aggression. To build trust, case management was used to demonstrate immediate outcomes and actions, such as a dental referral.A Self-Sufficiency Matrix was used to identify the client's top three priority needs and assess their level of insight and self-awareness. Using the FISH model, foundational needs were addressed first to stabilise the client and reduce their crisis state.Upcoming legal matters were identified, including a significant risk of incarceration and no legal support. A referral was made to our collaborating lawyer and a support letter outlining the client's treatment plan was provided to assist with court proceedings.Harm-reduction strategies and psychoeduc

title: Supporting Overlooked Mental Health and Housing Needs
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for Free Supporting Overlooked Mental Health and Housing Needs 2 minutesTraining & PDA supported intervention for a client with complex mental health needs and housing insecurity — resulting in formal assessment, stabilised housing, and successful transition into part-time employment.BackgroundThe client presented with long-term mental health concerns, including anxiety, depression, and suspected OCD and ADHD/ASD. Despite being highly compliant and engaged with services, they had made little progress and were frequently overlooked due to being assessed as "low priority."The client had a history of long-term unemployment, minimal work experience, insecure housing, and limited financial stability. Family supports were limited, with complex interpersonal dynamics. Previous diagnoses had been suggested but not followed up or actively managed.Our ApproachFoundational sessions and an assessment matrix were completed to accurately assess the client's self-awareness, motivation, and functional capacity. Medical summaries were requested from current and previous providers to support a comprehensive understanding of the client's baseline presentation.A secondary consultation was completed by an accredited mental health social worker, who was able to provide an assessment for OCD. A tailored treatment plan was developed to ensure therapeutic approaches aligned with, rather than exacerbated, the client's mental health challenges.Housing insecurity was addressed through case management support. This included assistance with a PREP application, negotiating a payment plan with the landlord, and liaising with tenant advocacy services.Counselling sessions focused on improving self-esteem, interpersonal skills, and boundary setting. As stability improved, the client was supported to explore employment options.The TransformationImproved understanding and management of mental heal

title: Coordinated Care for High-Risk Family and Trauma Needs
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeCoordinated Care for High-Risk Family and Trauma Needs 2 minutesTraining & PDAn intensive coordinated intervention for a client with complex trauma, family violence, and child protection involvement — resulting in reunification, voluntary care engagement, and a pathway back to study.BackgroundThe client presented with high and complex needs, requiring involvement from multiple services. There were significant safety concerns, including ongoing or historical family violence, child protection involvement, and complex trauma.The client had a background of CPTSD and trauma, with shallow engagement and compliance across services. While multiple supports were involved, gaps remained in treatment coordination, risk management, and therapeutic support.Our ApproachThe intake appointment was prioritised to assess immediate risk, safety concerns, and service needs. Intensive case management was implemented, with SAMMs actively contacting and collaborating with mandated services to confirm engagement and clarify roles.Weekly appointments and phone support were offered to meet the client's high and fluctuating needs. Where appropriate, outreach support was provided to reduce barriers to engagement.A comprehensive safety plan was developed collaboratively between the clinician and client, with after-hours crisis resources provided. SAMMs worked to increase the client's self-awareness, engagement, and accountability while maintaining a trauma-informed and strengths-based approach.OutcomesDetailed reports provided to relevant services outlining engagement, treatment, and progressChildren returned to the client's care, with child protection involvement shifting from mandated to voluntaryOngoing and consistent engagement with SAMMsPTSD assessment and tailored treatment plan provided by an accredited mental health social workerSuccessful application to return to study, su

title: Beyond Training: Embedding Trauma-Informed Practice in Organisational DNA
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.

The session explores leadership, policy, and relational culture as vehicles for long-term change. title: Reflective Supervision: Creating Safe Spaces for Growth
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsReflective Supervision: Creating Safe Spaces for GrowthMontana NicoloutsopoulosPolicyShareMontana reframes supervision as a compassionate space where professionals are held with the same care they offer others. This webinar outlines the core principles of reflective supervision, including containment, emotional regulation, and boundary awareness. title: The Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic Harm
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsThe Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic HarmSharon MamoEducationShareRepairing trust is a rhythmic process that requires acknowledgment, consistency, and care. In this session, Sharon discusses how organisations can rebuild relationships after harm by fostering transparency, empathy, and shared accountability. Drawing on SAMMS’ cross-sector experiences, she explores how reflective communication transforms apology into sustained, restorative practice that re-establishes confidence between services and communities.UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →AllLeadershipCommunityEducationSystemsReflectionPolicyCollaborationResearchInnovationPractice45:43FeaturedReframing Complexity: Trauma-Informed Practice in Modern SystemsSharon MamoInnovation26:54FeaturedRestoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex CareMontana NicoloutsopoulosCollaboration33:47Building Connection in Remote PracticeSharon MamoCollaboration41:15Leading with Care: Trauma-Informed Leadership in ActionMontana NicoloutsopoulosReflection36:20The Power of Story: Reconnecting Through Narrative PracticeSharon MamoSystems58:32Mapping the Nervous System: Practical Neuro-Informed Tools for CliniciansSharon MamoCommunity43:21Reflective Supervision: Creating Safe Spaces for GrowthMontana Nicolou

description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.

Montana presents contemporary examples of trauma-informed innovation emerging from collaborative partnerships and adaptive leadership. title: Research in Motion: Measuring the Impact of Trauma-Informed Change
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsResearch in Motion: Measuring the Impact of Trauma-Informed ChangeSharon MamoResearchShareWhat does it mean to evaluate trauma-informed work without reducing people to numbers? Sharon explores human-centred approaches to research and evaluation — prioritising stories, safety, and authenticity over traditional metrics. This webinar highlights participatory evaluation, outcome mapping, and reflective supervision as tools for measuring growth while maintaining respect for lived experience. Participants will gain practical insight into how to communicate impact in a way that feels both rigorous and relational.UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →AllLeadershipCommunityEducationSystemsReflectionPolicyCollaborationResearchInnovationPractice45:43FeaturedReframing Complexity: Trauma-Informed Practice in Modern SystemsSharon MamoInnovation26:54FeaturedRestoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex CareMontana NicoloutsopoulosCollaboration33:47Building Connection in Remote PracticeSharon MamoCollaboration39:17The Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic HarmSharon MamoEducation41:15Leading with Care: Trauma-Informed Leadership in ActionMontana NicoloutsopoulosReflection36:20The Power of Story: Reconnecting Through Narrative PracticeSharon MamoSystems58:32Mapp

title: Building Connection in Remote Practice
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsBuilding Connection in Remote PracticeSharon MamoCollaborationShareRemote and hybrid care models can magnify disconnection. This webinar offers practical, neuroscience-informed approaches to sustaining relational presence across virtual settings. Dr. Ng explores tone, rhythm, silence, and micro-moments of empathy — demonstrating how small acts of attunement preserve authenticity, cohesion, and care, even across screens.UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →AllLeadershipCommunityEducationSystemsReflectionPolicyCollaborationResearchInnovationPractice45:43FeaturedReframing Complexity: Trauma-Informed Practice in Modern SystemsSharon MamoInnovation26:54FeaturedRestoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex CareMontana NicoloutsopoulosCollaboration39:17The Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic HarmSharon MamoEducation41:15Leading with Care: Trauma-Informed Leadership in ActionMontana NicoloutsopoulosReflection36:20The Power of Story: Reconnecting Through Narrative PracticeSharon MamoSystems58:32Mapping the Nervous System: Practical Neuro-Informed Tools for CliniciansSharon MamoCommunity43:21Reflective Supervision: Creating Safe Spaces for GrowthMontana NicoloutsopoulosPolicy52:10From Burnout to Balance: Supporting Clinicians in Complex CareSharon Mamo

title: Designing Systems for Humanity: Policy Meets Practice
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsDesigning Systems for Humanity: Policy Meets PracticeSharon MamoPracticeSharePolicy shapes how care is delivered — and too often, it’s built without the voices of those most affected. Dr. Youssef presents a framework for trauma-informed policymaking, connecting evidence-based practice to structural reform. Participants gain insight into reflective leadership, shared accountability, and ethical data use — designing systems that serve human need, not administrative order.UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →AllLeadershipCommunityEducationSystemsReflectionPolicyCollaborationResearchInnovationPractice45:43FeaturedReframing Complexity: Trauma-Informed Practice in Modern SystemsSharon MamoInnovation26:54FeaturedRestoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex CareMontana NicoloutsopoulosCollaboration33:47Building Connection in Remote PracticeSharon MamoCollaboration39:17The Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic HarmSharon MamoEducation41:15Leading with Care: Trauma-Informed Leadership in ActionMontana NicoloutsopoulosReflection36:20The Power of Story: Reconnecting Through Narrative PracticeSharon MamoSystems58:32Mapping the Nervous System: Practical Neuro-Informed Tools for CliniciansSharon MamoCommunity43:21Reflective Supervision: Creating Safe Spaces

title: The Power of Story: Reconnecting Through Narrative Practice
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsThe Power of Story: Reconnecting Through Narrative PracticeSharon MamoSystemsShareStorytelling is one of humanity’s oldest healing tools. This webinar explores how narrative practice helps individuals reclaim agency and coherence in their life stories. Anna guides participants through examples of metaphor and reframing, showing how stories shift the lens from pathology to meaning. Practitioners learn how to uncover deeper narratives beneath surface behaviour and create space for clients to re-author their own experiences.UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →AllLeadershipCommunityEducationSystemsReflectionPolicyCollaborationResearchInnovationPractice45:43FeaturedReframing Complexity: Trauma-Informed Practice in Modern SystemsSharon MamoInnovation26:54FeaturedRestoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex CareMontana NicoloutsopoulosCollaboration33:47Building Connection in Remote PracticeSharon MamoCollaboration39:17The Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic HarmSharon MamoEducation41:15Leading with Care: Trauma-Informed Leadership in ActionMontana NicoloutsopoulosReflection58:32Mapping the Nervous System: Practical Neuro-Informed Tools for CliniciansSharon MamoCommunity43:21Reflective Supervision: Creating Safe Spaces for GrowthMontana Nicoloutsop

title: Mapping the Nervous System: Practical Neuro-Informed Tools for Clinicians
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsMapping the Nervous System: Practical Neuro-Informed Tools for CliniciansSharon MamoCommunityShareUnderstanding the body’s adaptive systems is central to trauma-informed care. This session introduces clinicians to accessible neurobiological principles that deepen therapeutic engagement.Through demonstration and discussion, participants learn co-regulation, pacing, and grounding techniques that support both client and clinician nervous systems — sustaining safety and connection in every interaction.UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →AllLeadershipCommunityEducationSystemsReflectionPolicyCollaborationResearchInnovationPractice45:43FeaturedReframing Complexity: Trauma-Informed Practice in Modern SystemsSharon MamoInnovation26:54FeaturedRestoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex CareMontana NicoloutsopoulosCollaboration33:47Building Connection in Remote PracticeSharon MamoCollaboration39:17The Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic HarmSharon MamoEducation41:15Leading with Care: Trauma-Informed Leadership in ActionMontana NicoloutsopoulosReflection36:20The Power of Story: Reconnecting Through Narrative PracticeSharon MamoSystems43:21Reflective Supervision: Creating Safe Spaces for GrowthMontana NicoloutsopoulosPolicy52:10From Burnout to Balance:

title: From Burnout to Balance: Supporting Clinicians in Complex Care
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsFrom Burnout to Balance: Supporting Clinicians in Complex CareSharon MamoLeadershipShareFrontline work demands constant emotional energy, yet clinicians often neglect their own regulation in the process. This webinar invites participants to reflect on professional sustainability through a trauma-informed lens. Dr. Tull explores the neuroscience of stress, the role of supervision, and how to recognise early signs of burnout before it becomes systemic fatigue. The session offers frameworks for maintaining presence and care in complex environments.UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →AllLeadershipCommunityEducationSystemsReflectionPolicyCollaborationResearchInnovationPractice45:43FeaturedReframing Complexity: Trauma-Informed Practice in Modern SystemsSharon MamoInnovation26:54FeaturedRestoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex CareMontana NicoloutsopoulosCollaboration33:47Building Connection in Remote PracticeSharon MamoCollaboration39:17The Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic HarmSharon MamoEducation41:15Leading with Care: Trauma-Informed Leadership in ActionMontana NicoloutsopoulosReflection36:20The Power of Story: Reconnecting Through Narrative PracticeSharon MamoSystems58:32Mapping the Nervous System: Practical Neuro-Informed Tools for Cli

title: Restoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex Care
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsRestoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex CareMontana NicoloutsopoulosCollaborationShareThis webinar explores how trauma-informed frameworks reshape the housing landscape by focusing on connection over correction. SAMMS facilitators unpack the Housing-First approach, emphasising how stability and safety lay the foundation for long-term behavioural and emotional change. The discussion highlights the value of interagency collaboration, reflective supervision, and adaptive care models that prioritise human dignity within complex service systems.UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →AllLeadershipCommunityEducationSystemsReflectionPolicyCollaborationResearchInnovationPractice45:43FeaturedReframing Complexity: Trauma-Informed Practice in Modern SystemsSharon MamoInnovation33:47Building Connection in Remote PracticeSharon MamoCollaboration39:17The Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic HarmSharon MamoEducation41:15Leading with Care: Trauma-Informed Leadership in ActionMontana NicoloutsopoulosReflection36:20The Power of Story: Reconnecting Through Narrative PracticeSharon MamoSystems58:32Mapping the Nervous System: Practical Neuro-Informed Tools for CliniciansSharon MamoCommunity43:21Reflective Supervision: Creating Safe Spaces for GrowthMontana Nico

title: Australia’s first self-funded Complex Needs Clinical Service for Employment Services
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutServicesImpact HubResourcesContact UsTerms of ServiceEmergencyMake A ReferralEmergencyAboutServicesImpact HubResourcesContact UsTerms of ServiceEmergencyMake A ReferralAustralia’s first self-funded Complex Needs Clinical Service for Employment Services4 minutesbyMontana NicoloutsopoulosMedia releaseEmployment services reformComplex needsAustralia’s first self-funded Complex Needs Clinical Service designed specifically for Employment Services participants opens tomorrow in Sunshine, marking a major milestone for long-term unemployed people navigating the increasingly complex Centrelink system.The SAMMs Complex Needs Clinic is a first-of-its-kind clinical service built for people facing entrenched, high-risk and long-term barriers including mental health challenges, addiction, criminal justice involvement, disability, chronic illness, trauma and severe social instability.Located at 1/5 Devonshire Road, Sunshine, the clinic launches in the same community where Sharon Mamo, CEO of Creating New Pathways and founder of the SAMMs model, once faced the very barriers she now specialises in addressing. For Ms Mamo, this opening is both a full-circle moment and a turning point in how complex unemployment is understood and supported in Victoria.Built by lived experience, proven through clinical designMs Mamo and her daughter, Montana Nicoloutsopoulos, a Mental Health Social Worker and 2025 Australian Social Worker of the Year nominee, self-funded the research, development and build of the SAMMs model. Their mission was to create a clinical service that acts quickly, intelligently and compassionately for people repeatedly failed by overstretched systems and reduced to tick-box compliance.These are individuals who often feel isolated, misunderstood and unable to advocate for themselves. Many struggle to meet constantly shifting Centrelink requirements without the support, regulatory capacity or clinical scaffolding needed to understand what is expected of them. SAMMs respond

title: The Partnerships That Built SAMMs
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutServicesImpact HubResourcesContact UsTerms of ServiceEmergencyMake A ReferralEmergencyAboutServicesImpact HubResourcesContact UsTerms of ServiceEmergencyMake A ReferralThe Partnerships That Built SAMMs4 minutesbySharon MamoClinical innovationEmployment services reformComplex needsWhy SAMMs ExistsSAMMs exists because people inside the system saw the cracks and had the courage to ask for something better. We were invited to step in where traditional counselling, activation and short-term interventions had already failed the people they were meant to help.The Early WISE Employment CollaborationIn the early years, WISE Employment engaged SAMMs CEO, Sharon Mamo, and Director, Montana Nicoloustopoulos, to assess and respond to a clear service gap within their ex-offender and complex-needs caseloads, an area both organisations recognised as critical to reform.At WISE’s request, Sharon and Montana provided extensive clinical consultation, program redevelopment, and workforce guidance to help their sites respond safely and effectively to participants facing entrenched psychosocial and neurological barriers.Building the FrameworkOver more than two years, Creating New Pathways self-funded and delivered thousands of professional hours to the sector, including clinical program design, case reviews, staff consultation, and system improvement.During this time, SAMMs developed a comprehensive model of care that integrated neuroscience, trauma-informed practice, and behavioural regulation principles into employment servicing.These activities informed the creation of the SAMMs Clinical Framework model owned and governed by Creating New Pathways, and now implemented nationally.What the Early Results DemonstratedThe early collaboration demonstrated the value of embedding clinicians within employment systems, turning engagement into stability, and crisis into capacity.Ongoing Employment PartnersWe also acknowledge our ongoing partners, SYC, APM, Octec, and Uniting Vic.Tas who conti
